title: papaver dubium subsp. mairei
intro: Papaver dubium subsp. mairei, also known as the Pyrenean poppy, is a subspecies of the Papaver dubium species, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the poppy family (Papaveraceae). It is native to the Pyrenees mountain range between France and Spain. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can help to alleviate symptoms of insomnia, anxiety, and digestive problems, and also supports immune function and overall well-being.
